diagrams.title: Memory Layout of C Program
diagrams.description: Shows segments of memory in a C program: Code, Static/Global, Heap (grows upward), Stack (grows downward)
elements: Stack (function calls, local variables - grows downward)
diagrams.purpose: Understanding where dynamic memory allocation fits in program memory structure and how heap and stack interact.

diagrams.title: malloc() vs calloc() Comparison
diagrams.description: Visual comparison showing how malloc allocates single block of uninitialized memory vs calloc allocates zero-initialized contiguous blocks
elements: malloc(5*sizeof(int)): 5 integer spaces with garbage values
calloc(5, sizeof(int)): 5 integer spaces all initialized to 0
Both return pointer to first element
diagrams.purpose: Understanding the difference in initialization between malloc and calloc functions and their return values.

diagrams.title: Memory Leak Scenario
diagrams.description: Diagram showing how memory leak occurs when allocated memory is not freed before pointer reference is lost
elements: Step 1: malloc() allocates memory, pointer points to it
Step 2: Pointer is overwritten or goes out of scope
Step 3: Memory still allocated but no reference exists
Step 4: Memory cannot be freed - LEAK
Result: Heap exhaustion over multiple iterations
diagrams.purpose: Understanding what memory leak is, how it occurs, and why it should be prevented.

diagrams.title: Dangling Pointer Problem
diagrams.description: Shows how dangling pointer is created when memory is freed but pointer still references that location
elements: Original pointer points to valid memory
free(ptr) releases memory back to heap
Pointer still contains old address (now invalid)
Solution: Set ptr = NULL after free()
diagrams.purpose: Understanding dangling pointers, their dangers, and prevention through null assignment.
